rulings.Case Title: CHARLES DENHAM et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 11, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue II (all causes of action) and off calendar as to issues III (false representation) and IV (punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Denham was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claims against defendant. Plaintiffs¿ objections to defendant¿s exhibits B, C and D are sustained. Even if defendant¿s exhibits B, C and D were admissible, they do not negate an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claims against defendant. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their false representation cause of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 11, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Defendant Sears, Roebuck and Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant did not attempt to sustain its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. rulings.Case Title: DELMAR STEPHENS VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 11, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Defendant Kaiser Ventures LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 1 (Workers¿ Compensation) and denied as to issues 2 (negligence and premises owner/contractor liability) and 3 (strict liability). As to issue 1, plaintiff represents that he does not contend that defendant is liable for injuries arising out of his employment by defendant thus this issue is unopposed. As to issue 2, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his negligence and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action. Defendant¿s failure to attach to its moving papers plaintiff¿s factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support these claims precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of either of these causes of action. As to issue 3, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his strict liability cause of action. Defendant¿s failure to attach to its moving papers plaintiff¿s factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support this cause of action precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of either of this cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Mission Stucco Company¿S Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 11, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.¿s joinder in Mission Stucco Co.¿s motion to transfer venue is denied as untimely. Merlex Stucco failed to provide proper notice as required by C.C.P. 1005(b) and 1010.6. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
